{"id":"project-LICHEN-vodw","title":"Hardcoded 256 in zcbor_tstr_put_term inconsistent with max length constants","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/senml/senml.c
Lines: 195, 211, 219

CATEGORY: API MISUSE

zcbor_tstr_put_term() is called with hardcoded max_len of 256:
  zcbor_tstr_put_term(state, pack->base_name, 256)
  zcbor_tstr_put_term(state, rec->name, 256)
  zcbor_tstr_put_term(state, rec->unit, 256)

But the defined constants are:
  #define SENML_MAX_NAME_LEN 32  (senml.h:57)
  #define SENML_MAX_UNIT_LEN 8   (senml.h:60)

While validation happens before encoding (so strings should already be within limits), this creates:
1. Inconsistency - if validation is ever bypassed, 256 bytes could be scanned
2. Maintenance hazard - if constants change, hardcoded values won't match
3. Wasted cycles - zcbor will scan up to 256 bytes unnecessarily

FIX: Use the constants:
  zcbor_tstr_put_term(state, pack->base_name, SENML_MAX_NAME_LEN + 1)
  zcbor_tstr_put_term(state, rec->name, SENML_MAX_NAME_LEN + 1)
  zcbor_tstr_put_term(state, rec->unit, SENML_MAX_UNIT_LEN + 1)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:51:46.176634Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:51:46.176659Z"}
